summary: High‑energy show with standout St. Stephen and a killer second set, captured in a good‑quality matrix/audience recording.
review: The concert is remembered as one of the hottest Dead nights, driven by raw stage energy and memorable moments like a breakout Wang Dang Doodle, soaring Jack Straw, Birdsong, Bertha and a rare Phil‑sung China Doll. Reviewers praise the entire pre‑drums run and note that while vocals were occasionally ragged, the performance more than makes up for it; the recording is considered solid enough (matrix or audience) to convey that excitement.

ai_rating.rationale: The single 5‑star review emphasizes an exceptional performance with many standout songs and only minor, non‑technical complaints; recording quality is described as adequate for a matrix/audience source.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No specific audio flaws mentioned; reviewer recommends listening to a matrix or audience mix, implying the existing recording is at least clear enough to enjoy the high energy of the show.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set packed with high‑energy highlights; second set builds even higher, especially the pre‑drums run and Bertha, delivering a nonstop rock‑filled momentum.

band_member_comments.Phil: Phil sang on China Doll (using his own mic), a rare occurrence at the time.
